events.text: Patient is a 62-year-old male of White/Caucasian race with an ECOG performance status of 1
At age 62, patient presented with rapidly enlarging cervical and mediastinal lymphadenopathy, night sweats, and unintentional weight loss of 15 pounds over two months
At age 62, excisional biopsy of a right cervical lymph node demonstrated diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, ICD-O site C77.9, histology code 9680/3

At age 62, pathology report from excisional lymph node biopsy showed diffuse proliferation of large atypical lymphoid cells with high mitotic rate, Ki-67 of 80 percent, immunohistochemistry positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, BCL6, negative for MUM1, consistent with germinal center B-cell (GCB) subtype by Hans algorithm
At age 62, flow cytometry on lymph node specimen showed a monoclonal B-cell population positive for CD20, CD19, CD10, and BCL6, negative for CD5, CD23, and MUM1
At age 62, FISH analysis showed no MYC rearrangement, no BCL2 rearrangement, no BCL6 rearrangement; classified as non-double-hit DLBCL
At age 62, laboratory workup revealed markedly elevated LDH at 485 U/L, CBC showed WBC 9.2, hemoglobin 11.8 g/dL, platelets 198, CMP within normal limits except albumin 3.2 g/dL

At age 62, staging PET/CT demonstrated intensely FDG-avid bilateral cervical, mediastinal, and para-aortic lymphadenopathy with the largest cervical node measuring 6.2 cm, SUVmax 22.4, no extranodal involvement identified
At age 62, bilateral bone marrow biopsy was negative for lymphoma involvement, showing trilineage hematopoiesis with no morphologic or immunophenotypic evidence of disease
At age 62, patient was staged as Ann Arbor Stage III with B symptoms, IPI score 3 (high-intermediate risk) based on age over 60, elevated LDH, and Stage III disease
At age 62, patient initiated cycle 1 of R-CHOP: rituximab 375 mg/m2, cyclophosphamide 750 mg/m2, doxorubicin 50 mg/m2, vincristine 1.4 mg/m2 capped at 2 mg, and prednisone 100 mg daily for 5 days
At age 62, following cycle 1 of R-CHOP, patient developed grade 2 nausea and grade 1 fatigue managed with ondansetron and supportive care
At age 62, patient received cycle 2 of R-CHOP with prophylactic pegfilgrastim, tolerated without significant complications
At age 62, patient received cycle 3 of R-CHOP on schedule with no dose modifications

At age 62, interim PET/CT after 3 cycles of R-CHOP showed significant reduction in FDG uptake across all previously noted sites, largest cervical node decreased to 2.1 cm, Deauville score 3, consistent with partial metabolic response
At age 62, patient received cycle 4 of R-CHOP without dose modifications
At age 62, after cycle 4, patient reported grade 1 peripheral neuropathy with tingling in fingertips attributed to vincristine
At age 62, patient received cycle 5 of R-CHOP, vincristine dose maintained as neuropathy remained grade 1
At age 62, patient completed cycle 6 of R-CHOP without significant adverse events
At age 62, end-of-treatment PET/CT demonstrated complete metabolic response with no residual FDG-avid lymphadenopathy, Deauville score 2, consistent with complete remission
At age 62, patient achieved complete remission after 6 cycles of R-CHOP and was transitioned to surveillance with clinical visits and CT imaging every 3 months
At age 63, surveillance CT of chest, abdomen, and pelvis showed no evidence of recurrent lymphadenopathy or new lesions, consistent with continued complete remission
At age 63, follow-up CT scan at 6 months post-treatment remained negative for recurrence
At age 64, patient remains in complete remission at 18-month follow-up with normal LDH, surveillance imaging transitioned to every 6 months

scenario_blurb: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), germinal center and activated B-cell subtypes. Ann Arbor stage II-IV. Treated with R-CHOP. Include IPI scoring, PET-CT response assessment, some with relapse requiring R-ICE salvage and autologous stem cell transplant.
